tutorialscreen_category_construction_des: Discover the tools to build your unique creations.
tutorialscreen_category_flumecreation_de: Tips on building a better flume.
tutorialscreen_category_guests_descripti: Get to know your guests on a deeper level.
tutorialscreen_category_management_descr: Grow your park to new heights!
tutorialscreen_category_parkbasics_descr: Some quick tips on how to create your very own theme park.
tutorialscreen_category_pathandpoolbuild: Path and pool creation have a lot of overlapping functions and tools.
tutorialscreen_category_pools_descriptio: Ensure your pools make a splash in your park!
tutorialscreen_category_ridecreation_des: Tips on building a better ride.
tutorialscreen_category_staff_descriptio: Learn more about your staff members and how to manage them.
tutorialscreen_category_themeparkrides_d: Getting the most out of your rides.
tutorialscreen_description_advancedmovet:
Open the Advanced Move Tool from the Settings panel to help precisely place objects.

Choose between each specific axis to move your object into the perfect position.

You can also use the Advanced Rotate Tool in the same way for more precise rotation.
tutorialscreen_description_beltsandjets:
Flumes are not powered. They will rely on gravity to provide the energy that carries the guests around the track. 


A flume always starts with a drop. The higher the drop, the more speed your guests can pick up. 


Most flumes that use vehicles, like Mats or Inner Tubes, can use Water Jets and Conveyor Belts to create new Lift Hills and keep the fun going!


Tip! Check the Utilities tab to see how you can make hills for your flume.
tutorialscreen_description_blueprints:
A blueprint is a saved collection of objects. You can create a blueprint in many different ways: after a Multi-Select, by selecting a scenery group like an arrangement of flowers or even a row of houses, a giant coaster or flumes, a flat ride or even all of those things saved together.

Depending on what you have selected, you have different ways of creating your blueprint. Like the new blueprint button or the save blueprint/building/ride/flume in the Advanced Settings tab.

Once you click on a save/create blueprint button you will have the choice to update a previous blueprint or to save a new creation.
tutorialscreen_description_blueprintwork:
It's always great to share your creations with the world, or to download amazing works by other park managers! To upload a blueprint to the Frontier Workshop, you have to go on the My Workshop tab and then in Upload a Creation. Inside you can chose any of your blueprints or sandbox park.

You can now upload this blueprint either as a new creation or replace an existing one.

After that you can change the title, the description and as a new blueprint you can choose to make it public so that everyone can access it or private so that it’s access can be restricted. Then click on the Upload to Frontier Workshop et voila it's online!
tutorialscreen_description_brakes:
A coaster train needs enough momentum to make it all the way around the track and back to the station.


Too much momentum when approaching the station will cause sudden braking, extreme g-force, and your guests to have a bad time!


Brake sections can be added to your track in areas where you would like to slow the coaster cars down.

There are several different types of Brake sections available, each with their own adjustable speed and deceleration rates.


Tip! Trim Brakes can be used in areas of your track where the train is going too fast. They will slow the coaster car down a little without stopping it altogether.
tutorialscreen_description_buildingrides:
New rides can be found in the Rides Browsers.

Don't forget that, once built, a ride needs both an entrance and an exit.

Once the exit and entrance have been connected to the rest of the park, your guests will be able to ride it.
tutorialscreen_description_chainliftsand:
Roller coaster trains are not powered. They will rely on gravity to provide the energy that carries the coaster trains around the track.


A chain lift is a common way to start a coaster. The higher the chain lift goes, the bigger the drop can be, and the more speed the coaster train can pick up.


Some coasters use different launch mechanisms, such as hydraulics or electromagnets, to propel the coaster train forward.


Tip! If your train fails to make it around your track, try increasing the size of your first drop, the speed of the launch system, or reducing the overall track length.
tutorialscreen_description_changingrooms:
Changing Rooms provide a space for your Guests to change into their swimwear, so that they can use Pools and Flumes.


Guests can use Shops and Facilities in their swimwear, but will need to get changed back into their normal clothes to use Theme Park Rides and to go home.


Tip! Make sure you build enough Changing Rooms to meet Guests' demands!
tutorialscreen_description_cinematics:
If you're after a change of pace, try experiencing your park from a different perspective!

Select the Ride Camera or Guest Camera and choose between a Cinematic, First Person, or Interest view.

And if you want to experience a ride as if you were truly on it, select the First Person view or Seat View in the Ride Camera!
tutorialscreen_description_classicpaths:
The Classic Paths tool allows you to:


• Place Paths with a single action.

• Place Paths that are always a fixed length.

• Auto-adjust between placing Straight and Curved paths.


Tip! You can adjust the width, length, height and angle of your path in the InfoPanel.
tutorialscreen_description_coasterbuildi:
To prevent being stuck in the station, you should always make sure your coaster has a way of giving the train speed from the start, like a rising chain lift track.

Make your new coaster exciting by adding hills, twists, and turns to the track, before eventually circling back to the station. Sharp changes of direction create extreme Fear and Nausea, which can be uncomfortable for your guests. Reducing the coaster's starting speed, banking into turns, or  smoothing the track could help reduce Fear and Nausea.

The last thing you need to do before opening your coaster to the public is to test it for safety.
tutorialscreen_description_coastersmooth:
Coaster Smoothing is a great way to even out sudden direction changes on your track, reducing Fear and Nausea and making your coaster more comfortable for your guests to ride. 

When in Edit mode you can select the track pieces you would like to smooth and adjust the strength, direction, and type of smoothing.


You can smooth the Turn, Height, and Banking of the track… or even all three at once! 


Tip! Using the Smooth tool alongside your coaster Test results is a great way to identify and address any parts of your track that might be causing high levels of Fear and Nausea.
tutorialscreen_description_coastertestin:
Every coaster needs to be tested for safety before it can be opened.


If the test results show that the ride’s Fear, Nausea or g-force are too strong, your guests may be unwilling to ride!


In the Testing tab, you can check heatmaps from previous tests to see where a coaster needs adjusting. When you know the problem areas, you can rebuild or smooth out any track sections that are a bit too extreme.


Tip! Using the test results and heatmaps together is a great combination to figure out how to improve your coasters!
tutorialscreen_description_crimeandsecur:
Guests don't always behave themselves in your park, and can engage in a variety of crimes. Pickpockets will rob unsuspecting guests, while Vandals take their frustration out on your park's facilities. 


Tighten up security in your park to keep your guests safe! Hire Security Guards to chase down criminals. Put down Security Posts for your Security Guards to monitor specific areas. Place Security Cameras to watch out for and deter criminal activity.


Tip! Check the Security heatmaps to see where to focus your resources.
tutorialscreen_description_crimepickpock:
Pickpockets are individual Guests that will steal from other Guests in your park. Guests that have been robbed will notice, and will be Unhappy until the perpetrator is caught. 


When spotted by Security, Pickpockets will run away to evade your Security Guards. If caught, Pickpockets are fined and ejected from the park. Pickpockets that evade capture, however, are free to strike again. 


Tip! Keep your Security Guards' Morale high to ensure they can outrun those pesky pickpockets.
tutorialscreen_description_crimevandalis:
Groups of Guests that are Unhappy may engage in Vandalism. Vandals will deface bins, benches, vending machines, and even bigger facilities like toilets, changing rooms and body dryers. 


Vandalised objects must be repaired before Guests can use them again. Janitors will repair vandalised objects and facilities, or you can Replace them in the InfoPanel at additional cost.


Vandals that are caught are fined, and ejected from the park.
tutorialscreen_description_customisebrus:
Looking to make your paths more consistent?


Use the Customise Brush to easily paint your currently selected style over any existing paths or pools.
tutorialscreen_description_demolishbrush:
The Demolish Brush is a quick and easy way to delete Scenery, Rides, Paths and more.

Filters can be changed to control the size of the Brush and what will be deleted.

The Area Mode allows for quicker large scale changes, while the Pointer Mode gives more precision.
tutorialscreen_description_displaysequen:
Why not wow your guests by creating a spectacular special effects display?

First, place a Display Sequencer object and select "Edit Trigger Sequence" from its info panel.

Then, add Display Groups by selecting objects with animations or special effects, like fireworks.

Finally, adjust the timing of the trigger sequence events, then set the display schedule to choose when the display will activate.
tutorialscreen_description_duplicate:
Don't forget that you can use the Duplicate function to make copies of objects and blueprints.

You'll find Duplicate via the Object / Blueprint menu and also in the Radial menu.

Because both Duplicate and Advanced Move can be found in the radial menu, you're able to immediately use Advanced Move on a duplicated object.
tutorialscreen_description_efn:
Excitement, Fear, and Nausea are three key values that affect a ride’s Prestige.


Build Excitement by generating more speed on your coaster, adding direction and speed changes, or by making trains pass close underneath other parts of the track. Aim for a high Excitement rating!


Try to keep your coaster’s Fear rating at around 5. Any higher and the ride becomes uncomfortable for guests, which will lower the ride’s Prestige.


Excessive g-force and inversions cause Nausea. Try to keep your coaster’s Nausea number as low as possible.


Tip! Use Testing mode when building your coasters to make sure your EFN scores are just right!
tutorialscreen_description_entrancefees:
You can set the price of tickets for rides, or for your park overall. The Bargain Price is an excellent deal that will draw in guests whilst the Premium Price will maximise income if enough guests buy it!

Ticket prices for park itself can be set at the entrance gate. There are two ticket categories: Adult Entry Tickets and Child Entry Tickets.

Be aware, changing entrance fees will also change the Bargain Price of your actual rides.

If entrance fees are too high, it can put guests off of entering the park at all.
tutorialscreen_description_facilities:
Facilities provide important infrastructure to meet Guest Needs and keep Guests spending their time and money in your park. 


Food Shops, Drink Shops, Souvenir Shops and Toilets are Facilities that meet Guest Needs. Guests that have their Needs met will spend more time in your park.


Guest Services provides your Guests with important park services like Pool Passes, Priority Passes, Umbrellas and Suncream.


Staff Buildings provide a space for your Staff members to take Breaks and raise their Morale between working hours.
tutorialscreen_description_flumedrops:
The first drop is very important, as this is where your guests will gain the momentum they need to make it all the way around your flume.  


Make sure your flume exits the first drop smoothly, as this drop can create a lot of longitudinal g-force! 


Tip! Be wary of adding sharp turns near the end of the first drop, as these can make guests uncomfortable, or send them flying when Enhanced Guest Flume Behaviours is toggled on!
tutorialscreen_description_flumeefn:
Excitement, Fear, and Nausea are three key values that determine a ride’s Prestige.


Build Excitement by generating more speed on your flume, adding direction or speed changes. Aim for a high Excitement rating!


Try to keep your flume's Fear rating at around 5. Any higher and the ride becomes uncomfortable for guests, which will lower the ride’s Prestige.


Excessive and prolonged g-force, especially lateral g-force, causes Nausea. Try to keep your flume's Nausea number as low as possible.


Tip! Flumes naturally have lower Excitement ratings than coasters. Check the Testing tab to keep an eye on your flume's EFN scores!
tutorialscreen_description_flumefiltrati:
Flumes and pools will get dirty as your guests continue to use them.


To clean them, make sure that your flumes are covered by an active Water Filter. Filters keep all water in their effective radius nice and clean!


To do this, connect Pumps and Filters together using Pipes. Your park entrance will provide water to get you started.


Tip! Make sure the Flume Platform your ride starts from is serviced by a filter, as this will keep the whole flume clean!
tutorialscreen_description_flumegforce:
G-force determines what kind of experience your guests will have when riding a flume.


High speeds from steep drops can be essential in giving your guests the momentum they need to get to the end of a flume.


Try going slower around sharp turns and some special pieces, as they turn this momentum into an unpleasant g-force!


G-forces are needed to generate Excitement, but excessive g-force can cause rapid Fear build-up! 


Tip! G-force can be viewed in real-time in a ride’s info panel, or through the heatmaps in the Ride Testing Tab.
tutorialscreen_description_flumeplatform:
Join multiple Flume Platforms together to make one big platform!


Larger platforms will let you add more and bigger flume types.


However, be careful not to overcrowd your platform with flumes, as each platform only has one entrance.


Tip! Keep an eye on your queue length to decide how many flumes to attach to a platform. Guests don't like waiting!
tutorialscreen_description_flumesmoothin:
Smoothing is a great way to even out sudden direction changes on your flume, reducing Nausea and making your flume more comfortable for your guests to ride. 


When in Edit mode you can select the flume pieces you would like to smooth and adjust the strength, direction, and type of smoothing you wish to use. 


You can smooth the Turn and Height of the flume... or both at once! 


Tip! Using the smooth tool alongside your flume Test results is a great way to identify and address any parts of your flume that might be causing high levels of Nausea.
tutorialscreen_description_flumetesting:
Every flume needs to be tested for safety before it can be opened.


If the test results show that the ride’s Fear, Nausea or g-force are too strong, your guests may be unwilling to ride! 


You can check heatmaps from unsuccessful tests to see where a flume needs adjusting. When you know the problem areas, rebuild or smooth out any flume sections that are a bit too extreme. 


Tip! Using test results and heatmaps together are a great way to figure out how to improve your flumes.
tutorialscreen_description_flumeturns:
Adding turns to your flume is important to make sure your guests have fun!


However, sharp turns in your flume can cause the g-force to spike, which is unpleasant for your guests.  


To automatically soften sharp changes in flume direction, you can use the Smooth Tool. You can also manually make the turn wider to lessen its effect.


Tip! When Enhanced Guest Flume Behaviours is on, sharp turns can cause your guests to fall off a flume, so make sure your turns are safe!
tutorialscreen_description_g-forces:
There are several types of g-force that guests will experience on your rides.


Lateral forces push guests sideways.


Vertical forces push guests upwards or downwards.


Longitudinal forces push guests forwards and backwards.


G-force is needed to generate Excitement and Fear, but excessive g-force can cause rapid Fear build-up!


Tip! G-force can be viewed in real-time in a ride’s info panel, or through the heatmaps in the Ride Testing Tab.
tutorialscreen_description_gamespeed:
The speed that time moves at in your park can be changed. Your options are 1x speed (normal speed), 2x speed, 5x speed, or you can pause time altogether!

Slower speeds will give you more time to build and improve coaster, rides, or flumes, and will make it easier to see how guests are enjoying your park.

Faster speeds are great for seeing how your park performs over a longer time period.

But be careful! Speeding up time can both make AND lose money at a faster rate depending on if your park is profitable or not.
tutorialscreen_description_gettingstarte:
Start by placing a Station. You can change which side of the platform to place your Entrance and Exit gates in the Station tab. Select the Chain Lift piece from the Utilities tab. Increase the angle and place several sections of Chain Lift track pieces to create your Lift Hill. At the top of your hill, flatten the angle back to horizontal. This way, your chain lift will pull the cars over the top of the hill. 


Switch to the Standard track piece and decrease the angle, creating your first drop! At the bottom of the drop, change the angle of the track to build some corners. Don’t make them too sharp! You can add Special track pieces to make your coaster more exciting. Build your track back to the opposite end of the station to complete the circuit. The Autocomplete tool can do this for you.

Start by placing a Flume Platform. Build it high above the ground to give your ride somewhere to go! Your flume will have to start with enough of a drop to give your guests the required momentum to travel the flume's length. After your drop, flatten the angle and add some turns to keep your guests engaged! Each flume type offers different Special pieces to make your flume stand out. Check the Specials tab to see what your flume can do!


When your guests have had their fun, your flume needs to end over a pool or into a runout lane to stop safely. Make sure that your flumes and pools are filtered. Go to the Filtering tab to check your water filter coverage. Don't forget to add an Entrance to your Flume Platform! Placing an entrance will automatically add stairs up to the platform's height.
tutorialscreen_description_guestnausea:
Sometimes, guests will go on rides that are too much for them to handle.
 
Any ride with a high nausea value will make guests sick for a period of time.

Balance this out by spacing your high nausea rides further apart.
tutorialscreen_description_guestneeds:
Guests' Moods and what they want to do in your park are affected by their Needs.


There are 11 Guest Needs to consider: Entertainment, Food, Drink, Energy, Toilet, Nausea, Panic, Souvenir, Warmth, Suncream and Dryness.


Keep Guests in your park for longer by catering to their Needs.
tutorialscreen_description_guestpanic:
Panic happens in 2 situations:  

- Panic in a pool
- Panic after a ride

When a guest goes on too many scary rides in quick succession, their Panic need will go down. When it gets low enough, guests will panic and other members of their group will try to comfort them. 

When a guest is in a pool that isn’t being looked after properly by lifeguards, their Panic need will also go down. If a guest panics in a pool, they'll need to be saved by a lifeguard!
tutorialscreen_description_guests:
Your park isn't a park without Guests!

Each guest is either a Child, a Teenager, or an Adult. As you would expect, different age groups enjoy different types of rides.

Guests may enjoy thrilling, exciting or gentle rides and they may want to enjoy your pools and flumes. They may even prefer specific food and drink brands.
tutorialscreen_description_guestservices: The Guest Services facility sells important items, including: Pool Passes, Priority Passes, Umbrellas and Suncream.
tutorialscreen_description_guestsunburn:
When exposed to the sun, Guests' Suncream Need will drop.


Check a Guest's Suncream Need level through the Heatmap. Guests that reach a critical level will become Sunburnt, which will make them Uncomfortable. 


There are 2 ways to deal with Sunburn:

- Place down a Guest Services shop that sells suncream.

- Place shelter over ride queues and high-traffic areas of the park so that guests are not exposed to the sun for too long.
tutorialscreen_description_hillsandairti:
Hills in your track can generate moments of airtime.


Airtime is when the vertical g-force is at zero. In other words, your guests feel like they are floating! 


The faster the train is travelling, the smaller the hill you need to achieve airtime.


Hills that descend steeply for long periods generate lots of momentum and g-force at the end of the drop. Make sure the exits from these drops are smooth and gradual to avoid making your guests uncomfortable. 


Tip! Hills are also good for slowly reducing speed as your coaster train climbs them.
tutorialscreen_description_loans:
Loans can be a great way to get on track to growing your park, but you have to be careful...

The amount you can borrow is determined by your Park Value and you will need to pay back more than you borrow.

The closer you are to the maximum you can borrow, the larger the Sales Cut will be.

The Sales Cut is the percentage of your money from sales which will automatically be taken to pay off the loan.

It's possible to pay back some of your loan early to reduce the Sales Cut, so long as you can spare the money.
tutorialscreen_description_maintenance:
Keeping a ride regularly serviced by a Mechanic will give you the green Serviced badge, this will ensure it doesn't break down.

If a ride is not serviced frequently enough, Ride Condition will start to deteriorate permanently.

Critically low Ride Condition can lead to breakdowns and require repairs.

Ride Condition can only be properly restored with a Refurbishment.
tutorialscreen_description_mechanicworks:
The Mechanic Workshop generates Research Points.


However, a Workshop can only generate Research Points when a Mechanic is assigned to it.


Simply assign a Mechanic to a Workshop and they'll start brainstorming new ways to entertain your guests!


Research Points unlock new rides and ride types.


Head to the Research help screen to learn more.
tutorialscreen_description_movementangle:
You can use Move Snap and Angle Snap to set fixed intervals for an object's position or orientation.

Global Snap allows you to move an object relative to the entire park.

Local Snap allows you to move an object relative to its own current orientation.
tutorialscreen_description_multiselect:
Multi-Select allows you to move, duplicate or delete multiple objects at once.

In Multi-Select mode, you can add or remove objects from the multi-selection at any time.

Use Multi-Select to create groups, as well as new Blueprints.
tutorialscreen_description_oswaldeugenec:
Park Complexity measures the total sum complexity of all elements in your park. This includes rides, pools, scenery, facilities and paths. If your park reaches 100% complexity, you will no longer be able to add any new elements until your park's complexity is reduced.

You can always check the Park Complexity Meter in the settings menu to see how you're doing.
tutorialscreen_description_parkareas:
The land to build your park on is divided into Areas. Go to the Area Overview when you want to know the full potential of your park!

Areas not already Owned are either Purchasable or Locked. 

A Purchasable Area is up for sale! Save up enough money and you’ll be able to expand your park.

A Locked Area will be unlocked once you earn enough medals. Then it becomes either Owned or Purchasable.

If an Area is Purchasable or Owned, you'll be able to see any Building Restrictions associated with it. Make sure to check whether an area is suitable for your ideas before you try to build!
tutorialscreen_description_pools:
Jump into Water Park Management and build some Pools!

Whether building blueprints or starting from scratch, Pools are endlessly customisable in shape and style.

Guests who love water parks will be drawn to any Pool and will happily spend time riding Water Flumes and Slides.
tutorialscreen_description_pooltoys:
Guests love to use pool toys and inflatables when they get into pools.

Pool toys are bought from the Splash Emporium, satisfying their Souvenir need.

Happy guests will spend more money, so make sure you're selling pool toys near your pools!
tutorialscreen_description_preferences:
Some guests will have their own personal preferences.

Guests will be more interested in anything that matches their preferences, and will even spend more on those things than normal!
tutorialscreen_description_prioritypasse:
For a higher cost, some of your guests will buy a Priority Pass to enter a separate, faster queue for rides.

You can sell Priority Passes through the Guest Services facility.

Just select "Enable Priority Pass" on the Ride tab to build a Priority Pass queue, allowing guests with a Priority Pass to skip ahead of everyone else.
tutorialscreen_description_rainumbrellas:
A dry guest is a happy guest, so be sure to keep your guests covered when the rain starts!


Unsheltered guests will see their Dryness Need plummet, which can make them Uncomfortable and want to leave your park.


Put down Body Dryers for wet guests, Guest Services shops to sell umbrellas, or make shelters to shield your guests from the rain.
tutorialscreen_description_restaurants:
Restaurants are customisable facilities that satisfy your Guests' Food and Drink needs.


To build your own Restaurant, place down a Restaurant Kitchen and then connect Picnic Benches or Dining Tables to it. Make sure you have a Vendor to serve your Guests, and a Janitor to clean up after them.


You can toggle which dishes your Restaurant serves in the Menu. Be sure to offer a variety of foods, so that your Restaurant caters to your Guests' Dietary Restrictions. Guests require multiple ingredients of differing types to completely satisfy their Food and Drink Needs.
tutorialscreen_description_rideage:
A ride's Reputation changes over time. Guests will start out excited about New rides, but will lose enthusiasm as they become Established, and then Dated.


Once they are Dated, rides with low Prestige will then become Aging, before eventually settling at Old status. These rides will be less popular with guests. 


Rides with high Prestige, however, will attain the Resurging status, before becoming Classic. These rides will be the talk of your park!


Tip! Statuses are not set in stone - keep an eye on your ride's Prestige to avoid your Classic ride becoming Old, or to raise an Old ride to Classic!
tutorialscreen_description_rideprestige:
Ride Prestige determines how much guests will be willing to pay for a ride. There are a few factors that affect Prestige: 


Ride Experience is one factor of a ride's Prestige. A ride's EFN is the biggest contributor to Ride Experience. Coasters with excessive G-Forces and long inversions can negatively impact Ride Experience, lowering Prestige.


Scenery will also affect the Prestige level. Try placing more scenery close to your flat rides and ride tracks to increase their Prestige.


Tip! A ride's Prestige will affect its Reputation status - keep Prestige high to attain Classic status!
tutorialscreen_description_rideprofits:
To build a successful park you need money.

Rides and Shops will increase your income, but require power and staff to run which cost you money.

Increasing your prices can improve your income, as long as you can keep your ride busy and your guests happy!
tutorialscreen_description_ridetriggers:
If you really want to impress your guests, don't forget to add special events to your track rides!

Select "Create Trigger Sequence" in the Customise tab, then add triggers on the track where you want events to occur.

Then, connect Special Effect objects or Animatronics to the triggers.

Each object's effect or animation will start when the ride's train reaches the trigger, giving guests an extra thrill!
tutorialscreen_description_ridetypescoas:
Coasters are the highlight of any theme park!

A Coaster’s appeal is represented by its Prestige, which is determined by its EFN and Scenery Rating. The higher the Prestige, the greater number of guests that are attracted to it.

Coasters can be placed with Prebuilt Blueprints, or by creating a Custom Coaster!

You can change the number of trains on a Coaster, and their operational mode. This directly affects how many guests can ride the Coaster at once.

Coasters tend to be pricey, so make sure you have enough money to purchase one!
tutorialscreen_description_ridetypesflat:
Flat Rides are a staple of many a good theme park!

A Flat Ride’s appeal is displayed by its Prestige, which is determined by its Excitement, Fear and Nausea ratings combined with a Scenery Rating. The EFN of a Flat Ride is determined by its Ride Sequence.

Flat Rides can be placed with by choosing a Prebuilt Blueprint that contain scenery, or by selecting "Create Custom".
tutorialscreen_description_ridetypesflum:
Flumes and Slides are new to Planet Coaster 2!

Flumes and Slides need Flume Station Platforms to be built from. You can build single or multiple flumes on a single Flume Platform.

These attractions also need to be connected to Water Filtration.

Flumes and Slides construction is similar to coasters, except they need to end near a pool.
tutorialscreen_description_ridetypestrac:
Track Rides share a lot of similarities with Coasters.

A Track Ride's appeal is displayed by its Prestige, which is determined by its EFN and Scenery Rating.

Track Rides will go at a constant speed around the track. You can change that speed in the Operations menu.

Track Rides are also built similarly to coasters, but are more restricted in their track angles. Because of this, Track Rides tend to be more gentle experiences than coasters.
tutorialscreen_description_ridetypestran:
Transport Rides are a great way to help guests get around your park.

It's a good idea to build Stations at key locations that guests might want to visit.

Each Transport Ride can support multiple Stations where guests can get on and off. You can open or close individual Stations however you'd like if you want to direct your guests to a specific spot.

Transport Rides have high power costs, but they can help guests spend less time walking and more time buying things at your park.
tutorialscreen_description_scenery:
Don't forget to immerse your guests in magnificent surroundings!

You can place Scenery Blueprints from the Scenery Browser to improve the mood of your guests, as well as your park rating.

Additionally, improving the queue scenery of a ride will increase that ride's prestige and draw more guests over to it.

If you want to build your own scenery creations piece by piece, try the "Create Custom" option.
tutorialscreen_description_scenerybrush:
The Scenery Brush can be used to quickly place a larger number of scenery pieces. 

There are brush presets available to help you, but you can create and save custom brushes.
tutorialscreen_description_securityguard:
Security Guards keep watch for crime, and chase down criminals. 


Unassigned Guards will patrol your park, keeping an eye out for criminal activity. You can also assign a Security Guard to a Security Post, to keep them monitoring the surrounding area.


Security Guards will pursue criminals once spotted, and give chase if they attempt to flee. After a successful chase, Security Guards will eject criminal guests from your park.


Tip! The pace at which Security Guards can chase suspects is based to their Morale, so be sure to keep them happy!
tutorialscreen_description_shopmenus:
Food and drink shops allow you to customise their menus in two major ways: 

- Adding extra ingredients to each item
- Removing items from the menu

Adding extra ingredients will increase how much a guest will pay for an item, and how happy that item makes them. But it will also increase the cost of ingredients for that item, which will make the shop's running cost go up.

Having fewer items on the menu will reduce the shop's running costs, but also lowers how happy a guest will be when they buy from the shop.
tutorialscreen_description_specialflumep:
Special flume pieces allow you to quickly create iconic moments, like Figure 8s and loops!


Pieces like Oscillators and High Speed Pipes can increase guest Excitement, but going too fast for too long can spike guest Fear and Nausea too!


Flumes also offer special end pieces like Runout Lanes, or Plug Holes which corkscrew your guests in a large bowl before dropping them into a pool.


Tip! Make sure the ride is calmer for a short while after using a Special flume piece, to allow Fear and Nausea levels to reduce.
tutorialscreen_description_specialtrack:
Special track pieces allow you to quickly create iconic coaster moments, like loops and corkscrews!


Special track pieces can significantly boost Excitement, Fear, and Nausea, so be sure to use them carefully or you may overwhelm your guests.


Some special track pieces feature inversions that turn the rider upside down. This gives a big boost to Excitement and Fear, with only a small initial increase to Nausea. However, inversions that last too long will start to increase Nausea rapidly, so it is important to keep the durations short and spaced out!


Tip! Make sure the ride is calmer for a short while after using a Special track piece, to allow Fear and Nausea levels to reduce.
tutorialscreen_description_staffbreaks:
Staff working 4 or more hours in a day require a Break, or their Morale will decrease.


Staff take their Breaks in Staff Buildings. Make sure to have enough capacity in a nearby Staff Building to allow Staff to take their scheduled Breaks. 


Staff require a minimum of 5 in-game minutes in a Staff Building for their Break to count, and will leave the Staff Building after 10 in-game minutes to go to their next work assignment.
tutorialscreen_description_staffentertai:
Entertainers walk around your park and interact with Guests, satisfying their Entertainment need.


Some Entertainers wear branded costumes, which direct nearby Guests to the shop brand that they are advertising.
tutorialscreen_description_staffhiringfi:
Staff can be hired in the Staff Tab of the Browser. Some facilities will Auto-Hire Staff when they are opened.


Staff can be fired via the Staff member's InfoPanel, or on the Staff Management screen. Fired Staff will stop receiving pay and leave the park immediately.


Tip! Fired Staff can't be re-hired, so make your decision carefully!
tutorialscreen_description_staffjanitors:
Janitors keep your park looking clean and tidy.


They are responsible for cleaning Toilets, Changing Rooms, Bins, and Benches. They will also clear any rubbish and vomit around the park.


Guests will have a bad experience in a dirty park, so make sure you have enough Janitors to go around!
tutorialscreen_description_stafflifeguar:
Lifeguards keep your Guests safe in pools.


Assign Lifeguards to Lifeguard Chairs or Lifeguard Posts around your pools to increase Pool Safety.


Lifeguards will monitor a set area in front of their assigned Post or Chair, and will help Guests who panic within that area.
tutorialscreen_description_staffmechanic:
Mechanics keep your Rides and Facilities working as intended. Rides, Generators, Water Pumps, and Transport Stations are all serviced and repaired by your Mechanics. 


An available Mechanic will automatically head over to a Ride or Facility in need of Repair or Service.


Tip! You can also manually request a Mechanic to Service or Refurbish a Ride in the InfoPanel.
tutorialscreen_description_staffmorale:
Staff Morale shows how happy Staff are to work in your park. Morale is calculated at the end of each day. Staff can be Very Happy, Happy, Content, Unhappy or Miserable. 


To increase Morale, set High Pay for Staff, and schedule Breaks in shifts of 4 hours or more. Happier Staff will move and work faster.


Giving Staff Low Pay, and refusing Breaks, will reduce Morale. Unhappy Staff will move and work slower. If a Staff member is Miserable for two days in a row, they will quit.
tutorialscreen_description_staffpay:
Staff Pay determines how much money your Staff receive for working in your park.


Different Staff types have different rates of Pay, which can be controlled by the Pay Scale in the InfoPanel at any time. 


All Staff will start at Fair Pay, which has no effect on Morale. High Pay will increase Morale, and Low Pay will reduce it. An average of the Staff's Pay will be calculated at the end of the day to determine their Morale.
tutorialscreen_description_staffpoolsafe:
Pool Area Safety represents how safe an area of a pool is when monitored by a staffed Lifeguard Post or Chair. You can see your pool's average Pool Area Safety in the InfoPanel.


When your Lifeguard Chairs and Posts are staffed, Pool Area Safety remains at 100%. However, this value will begin to drop when a Lifeguard leaves or goes on break. 


Tip! Guests will panic less in an area with higher Pool Area Safety, so ensure your pool has adequate Lifeguard coverage.
